per_question.question: How does the system handle order status history tracking?
per_question.expected_answer: Order status changes are tracked in the tblOrderStatusHistory table, which creates an audit record for each status transition. Each history record includes OrderID, OldStatus, NewStatus, ChangedByUser, ChangedDate, and ChangeReason fields, providing a complete audit trail of order workflow.
per_question.generated_answer:
The system handles order status history tracking through the business concept called Audit log of order status changes, which is implemented by the physical table TBLORDERSTATUSHISTORY / tblOrderStatusHistory.

This table stores one history record per status change. Its purpose is explicitly an audit log of order status changes. Each record includes HistoryID as the primary key, OrderID as the foreign key to VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID, OldStatus as the previous status value, NewStatus as the new/current status after the change, ChangedByUser as the username who made the change, ChangedDate as the timestamp of the status change, and ChangeReason as free text for the reason, such as cancellation reasons.

The tracking works by linking each status-change record back to the sales order header through OrderID -> VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID. That means to trace an order’s status history, you would join tblOrderStatusHistory.OrderID to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID and then review the sequence of ChangedDate values. OldStatus can be NULL for the initial status, which indicates the first recorded status assignment for an order. ChangedDate defaults to GETDATE(), so the system automatically timestamps each change when the history row is inserted. ChangedByUser references User.UserName, so the audit trail captures who made the change as well as when and why.

The current order status itself appears on VW_SALESORDERHDR as strOrderStatus, while tblOrderStatusHistory preserves the historical transitions between statuses over time. In the migration guidance, tblOrderStatusHistory is classified as a secondary-priority table and described as the order status audit.
